ui.monster_stats: {name} Stats: CR {cr} | AC {ac} | HP {hp} | STR {strength} | DEX {dexterity} | CON {constitution}
ui.save_read_error: The save file could not be read.
ui.unknown_chapter: Your save is at an unknown chapter.
choice.move_orc_camps: Move on to the Orc Camps
choice.use_keys_chest: Use the keys to open the chest
choice.loot_chests: Loot! Let's see what's in those chests!
choice.pour_ale: Pour dwarven ale down its throat
choice.dm_orcs:
Orcs and Goblins. 
Let's see how they fare
choice.dm_hydra:
Fuck dem kids. 
 Send the Hydra
choice.dm_barracks: Barracks. Let them suffer with discipline.
choice.dm_merchant: Merchant District. Shopping episode, but terrible.
choice.dm_barracks_armory: The armory door squeaks like hell
choice.dm_barracks_quarters: The living quarters have a surprise
choice.dm_merchant_combat: Straight to combat. I am busy.
choice.dm_manor: Large Manor. Let them chase shiny things.
choice.dm_manor_orb: Let them find the magistone orb
choice.dm_mimic_burn: They burn the house. Rude but smart.
choice.dm_mimic_loot: They loot the chests. Yes. Good.
choice.dm_mimic_rest: They rest here. Oh no, buddy.
choice.drag_out: Drag them out slimed and alive
story.login: Login or play as a guest
story.start:
Welcome to Tales of Visteria.

A story DM'd by Jubikino
The world of Visteria was created by MolarCapybara
An adaptation made by Redxjak

Content warning: vulgar language and dark fantasy themes.

Choose New Game to begin, or Load Game to continue a saved journey.
story.dm_intro:
You look down from above at the puny mortals whose fate you ultimately control. You see them in a caravan and decide they need some excitement.

What trouble do you send?
story.dm_send_hydra:
You send the hydra.

Haha! Did you see the way their eyeballs popped from their skulls? 
Then the smell of their burning flesh, ah that was great... 

I should make some popcorn.
story.dm_send_orcs:
Wow, they ran like little bitches, but they survived. Fine, I guess I get to keep playing with their lives.

Do I let them go to the caves of darkness and despair, or the forest of life, gifts, and power?
story.dm_choose_forest: Forest it is. Gifts, power, probably psychological damage. The classics.
story.dm_choose_caves: Caves was always the option. The caves are really the whole story.
story.dm_ghost:
Alright, what are these fuck tards going to do now? Let's give them this creepy-ass ring girl and see what they do.

Who do I want to watch?
story.dm_watch_cletus_low: Cletus does not think. He just rushes to the ghost girl, tries to swing his axe, and falls on his ass. Hahahaha, I cannot believe he did that.
story.dm_watch_cletus_high: Cletus is actually good with kids and convinced her to go "play" with the orcs coming in. Oh boy, I did not expect that
story.dm_watch_cal_low: Cal tried to sneak past her and tripped on his robe. Not that it mattered. She knew you were there, dude. Guess you are going to have to fight.
story.dm_watch_cal_high: Way to take the initiative, Cal! That magic missile to the chest is a quick way to take out a ghost.
story.dm_watch_ren_low: Ren, you moron. You have the best eyesight and can see it is a ghost, yet you decided to throw a fucking stick at it? Dude, you are so dead.
story.dm_watch_ren_high: Well, you talked to her like a dad and convinced her to help, so I guess that works. I am not sure how helpful it was for her to tell you about some "pointy sticks."
story.dm_watch_kili_low: Oh, dwarf guy. Kili, I think. I kind of forgot about you. Let's see what you d... dude, no fucking way. Did you really try to kiss the ghost? That is not your goth baddie!
story.dm_watch_kili_high: You are the only one who could have recognized that she was a 200-year-old princess and promised to save her kingdom. Nice work, dude.
story.dm_watch_continue: Alright, they got past the creepy dead kid. I guess I need to see where these idiots wander next.
story.dm_districts:
They make it to the old road sign.

Left goes to the barracks. Right goes to the merchant district.

Okay, this is where I get to be a little mean. Do I send them toward pointy sticks and angry orcs, or toward a market full of goblins who have absolutely never paid taxes in their lives?
story.dm_barracks:
The barracks are shaped like a colosseum because of course the dwarves built a training ground like they were compensating for something.

Three orcs patrol the center ring. There is an armory, living quarters, and a very obvious chance for someone to do something stupid.

What do I make interesting?
story.dm_barracks_armory_low: They trip over a shield. A shield. The thing made to stop people from getting hit is now the reason they are about to get hit. Beautiful.
story.dm_barracks_armory_high: They actually sneak into the armory. Fine. Let them feel competent for twelve seconds.
story.dm_barracks_quarters_low: They open the first door and find a goblin in a dress. He screams. I did not plan that, but I am absolutely keeping it.
story.dm_barracks_quarters_high: Empty room. Kind of boring, honestly, but not everything can be a haunted child or a murder chest.
story.dm_barracks_combat:
Three orcs notice them immediately.

I would say good luck, but I control the luck, so that feels dishonest.

Combat starts: three orcs.

Assuming they win, I will let them loot the bodies because I am generous and handsome and definitely not making that up.
story.dm_barracks_loot_low: Nothing. Perfect. They dug through a bunch of old military junk and found disappointment.
story.dm_barracks_loot_mid: A health potion. Practical, boring, and exactly the kind of thing they will forget to use until it is too late.
story.dm_barracks_loot_high: They find a map. Oh wow, look at them, solving my death bridge before they even get there.
story.dm_barracks_map:
They find a map showing the direct route to the throne room.

It also shows the safe bridge path: LEFT, UP, RIGHT, DOWN.

I made a whole trapped bridge and the nerds found instructions. Fantastic. I hope they forget it immediately.
story.dm_merchant:
They go right into the merchant district.

The market is wrecked, the stalls are dead, and five goblins with two orcs are wandering around like this is a field trip for ugly little criminals.

How do I want this to go?
story.dm_merchant_shop_low: The shop door squeaks so loud it might as well yell, "Hey monsters, free adventurers inside." Excellent door work.
story.dm_merchant_shop_high: The door opens quietly. The owner kept it well-oiled, which means this dead city has better maintenance than most living towns.
story.dm_merchant_sneak_low: A war horn sounds. They have been spotted. That is what happens when you try to sneak through a street like you are in a parade.
story.dm_merchant_sneak_high: They actually sneak past. No one sees them. I am proud and annoyed.
story.dm_merchant_combat:
Five goblins and two orcs notice them.

This is a lot of monsters, but they chose violence, and I respect bad decisions when they are funny.

Combat starts: five goblins and two orcs.

If they survive, I will let them loot the bodies.
story.dm_merchant_loot_low: They find gold, rotten fruit, and dwarven ale. That ale is either treasure or a war crime. Probably both.
story.dm_merchant_loot_mid: They find a health potion. Look at that, responsible adventuring. Disgusting.
story.dm_merchant_loot_high: They find a map. There goes my bridge trap mystery. I swear these people only roll high when it bothers me.
story.dm_merchant_map:
They find a map showing the direct route to the throne room.

It also shows the safe bridge path: LEFT, UP, RIGHT, DOWN.

I put the answer in the world because I am fair. I made the parchment crumble because I am also hilarious.
story.dm_residential:
They reach the residential district.

There are houses ahead, which means doors, and doors mean players will absolutely open the worst one available.

I have options.

Large Manor - crashing, yelling, and probably loot.
Small Shack - quiet, suspicious, and not worth the smell.
Large House - clean, perfect, full of chests, and definitely fine. So fine. Super normal.
Skip all that and go to the bridge.

What do I want to watch?
story.dm_manor:
The manor is loud inside. Something is crashing around, which is either monsters fighting or furniture having a nervous breakdown.

There is a black chest in there too, because nothing says "please open me" like a terrible idea with hinges.

Do I want to give them a real prize or a lesson?
story.dm_manor_orb:
They unlock the chest and find a pure magistone orb pulsing with power.

That thing is absolutely going to matter later. Or explode. Maybe both. I have not decided, and that is the joy of being God with snacks.
story.dm_manor_boom:
The black chest detonates.

That is what happens when you trust a mystery box in a dead city. Honestly, I feel like the chest was very clear about its whole vibe.
story.dm_shack:
They open the small shack and find a gremlin and a ghoul doing some kind of... ritual.

Nope. Not explaining that. Combat starts.

Fight setup: one gremlin and one ghoul.

If they win, there is nothing worth taking, unless someone wants emotional damage in a bag.
story.dm_mimic_house:
They open the big clean house.

It feels safe. It feels welcoming. It has chests sitting right there.

This is bait so obvious it should have a sign, which means someone will still bite it.
story.dm_mimic_burn:
They set the house on fire.

Honestly, fair. If a building feels too friendly in my campaign, burning it down is a valid form of insight.
story.dm_mimic_loot:
They open a chest and it has teeth.

Yes. There it is. That beautiful moment when treasure becomes dental work.

Fight setup: one mimic, then maybe two more if they get cocky.

If they survive the whole stupid house, I will drop a torn map piece with the first two bridge directions: LEFT, then UP.
story.dm_mimic_rest:
They sleep in the mimic house.

I almost feel bad. Almost.

The house turns into a mouth and starts eating them. This is what happens when you nap inside suspicious architecture.
story.dm_bridge:
They finally reach the bridge to the King's Castle.

Dead monsters are scattered across it, caught in old dwarven traps. Fantastic. Environmental storytelling and a warning label, all in one.

The safe route is LEFT, UP, RIGHT, DOWN.

If they found the full map, they had the answer and now have to remember it. If they only got the mimic scrap, they know LEFT and UP, then they get to enjoy panic. If they found nothing, well, that sounds like a them problem.

This is where I would stop the current Jon draft: right before the bridge choices start.
story.caravan:
Your caravan crawls through the dusk road to Visteria.
Then horns split the air. Goblins rush from the brush. Orcs break the line.
You fight beside the wagons until the guards are scattered and the road is lost.

Do you stand and fight, or run for the ravine?
story.caravan_fight: You plant your feet beside the burning wagons as an orc and two goblins push through the smoke.
story.caravan_combat_victory: The orc drops last, leaving the road briefly clear around you.
story.caravan_run: You dive through the burning canvas and sprint toward the ravine.
story.caravan_overrun: The caravan is overrun. Escape is the only thing left.
story.escape:
You are still being chased.
Ahead stands a faded signpost, its words scraped away by time.

One path leads into a forest with gray miasma. The other drops into a cave.
story.escape_forest:
The forest breathes a gray miasma.
Your thoughts loosen. Branches bend into familiar shapes.
After hours, you stagger back to the same blood-stained road, still being chased, as if no time passed.
story.escape_forest_repeat: The forest will not let you pass. The cave waits like a mouth.
story.escape_cave: You slip into the cave as shouts rise behind you.
story.cave:
The cave descends into an ancient buried city. Everything here is dead.
Behind you, goblin voices echo closer.

Do you fight them, or go deeper?
story.cave_fight_start: Two goblins scramble into the cave behind you, blades drawn and eyes wide in the dark.
story.cave_fight_success: You hold the narrow passage and drive them back.
story.cave_fight_fail: They overwhelm your guard before fleeing from something farther below.
story.cave_deeper: You hurry deeper until the voices fade.
story.ghost_girl:
Little girl with white face and black hair standing in the corridor.

What do you do?
story.persuade_mid:
She giggles, points to the merchant district and says "There's a fun glowy ball that way."

When you look back, she is gone and a doll is in her place.
story.persuade_high:
You tell her you are being chased and ask for her help. She disappears and drops a doll. You hear terrified screams of orcs and goblins.

You carefully, but swiftly, walk deeper, unwilling to turn back to the horrors you can only imagine going on to the monsters behind you.
story.ghost_black_pits_death: Her eyes turn to black empty pits of despair. You feel as if you are staring into a black hole that sucks you in. Nothing matters to you. Soon you lose your sense of self. You lose your life.
story.ghost_black_miasma: Black miasma like smoke pours from her and she disappears into it. Before long that disappears as well.
story.ghost_play: She looks at you and giggles "Play?"
story.fight_girl_warrior:
You let out a mighty war cry, raise your axe and run at the apparition, with the knowledge that nothing can stop you.

You aimed for the head and swung through it like an axe through air, and keep going. You pass completely through her and fall to the ground. Yup you were right, nothing could stop you.
story.fight_girl_scholar:
You feel for the swirling core of potential in your soul and feel the power there. With an extension of your will, you manifest it in a blast of power and hurl it at the unknown specter in front of you.

She lets out an ear-piercing scream that disorients you. You feel blood coming out of your ears and have the worst headache of your life.
story.fight_girl_ranger:
You nock an arrow and pull back on the bowstring. You aim carefully at the little girl with the precision of a hunter and release the string.

Your arrow passes through her as if nothing was there. Your keen eyes notice a slight distortion where the arrow flew through.
story.fight_girl_dwarf:
Damn, is that my goth baddie? I'm gonna give her a kiss.

You run as fast as your short legs can take you to your 17.50
story.sneak_past_girl_start: You try to slip past her without drawing attention.
story.sneak_past_girl_result: She turns her head and watches you, even through walls. Once you get to a point past her, she instantly appears in front of you.
story.doll_choice: You hear the monsters getting closer. Do you grab the doll, or just run?
story.doll_grab: You quickly grab the doll and start running.
story.doll_leave: You turn away from the doll and run. You feel a shiver and sneak a quick glance back. The doll is gone.
story.search_city_low: You find a few gold coins, some rotten fruit, and a bottle of dwarven ale.
story.search_empty: Everything has been picked clean, there is nothing here.
story.throne_map:
Map showing the direct route to the throne room, with instructions on how to avoid the traps.

The safe bridge path is: {directions}.
Remember it or write it down. The map is too brittle to keep checking later.
story.combat_potion: You drink a health potion and regain {healing} health.
story.combat_run_success: You break away from the fight and keep moving.
story.combat_run_fail: You try to run, but the monsters cut off your escape.
story.combat_dodge: You focus on defense and try to avoid the next blow.
story.combat_attack_roll: Attack {attack_number}: d20 roll {natural} + {bonus} = {total} to hit {enemy} AC {ac}.
story.enemy_hit: {enemy} rolls {natural}, total {total} against your AC {ac}, and hits you for {damage} damage.
story.enemy_miss: {enemy} rolls {natural}, total {total} against your AC {ac}, and misses.
story.combat_loot_map: Loot bodies to find a map.
story.level_up:
Level up! You reached level {level}.

Your maximum health automatically increases by 2.

Choose one reward.
story.level_hp: Your maximum health increases even more, and you feel tougher.
story.level_ac: You tighten your guard. Your armor class increases.
story.level_damage: Your strikes land harder. Your damage increases.
story.level_heal: You catch your breath and recover to full health.
xp.reason.doll_choice: made a choice about the doll
story.districts:
You get to the end of the road.
The sign points left to Barracks and right to the Merchant district.

Which route do you take?
story.districts_rest:
You find a sheltered corner near the old road sign and take a moment to breathe.

Your health is restored.
story.city_enter: You turn right toward the city district.
story.city_girl_hint: There's a fun glowy ball that way. The girl's whisper follows you between the old market stalls.
story.city_description:
You enter the city gates and find yourself in a market area, with some buildings that look like homes farther down the road.

A band of five goblins and two orcs roams nearby.
story.city_shop: You try to stay to the shadows and sneak into one of the open shops.
story.city_shop_fail: You reach for the door handle and push it open. A loud squeak echoes out into the city.
story.city_shop_success: You reach for the door handle and slowly push it open. It looks like the owner kept it well-oiled. Nice.
story.city_assault: You are immediately seen. Prepare for a fight.
story.city_sneak: You try to sneak past the monsters.
story.city_sneak_fail: You hear a war horn sound. You have been spotted.
story.city_sneak_success: You successfully avoid their gaze and sneak past them.
story.city_combat_victory: The last monster falls in the ruined market.
story.barracks_enter: You turn left toward the barracks.
story.barracks_girl_hint: Pointy sticks. The girl's whisper lingers in your head.
story.barracks_description: You make your way into the barracks. It is shaped like a colosseum. The outer ring holds an armory and living quarters, with a fighting pit in the center. Three orcs patrol the inner ring.
story.barracks_armory: You try to sneak to the armory.
story.barracks_armory_fail: You trip over a leftover shield. Prepare to fight.
story.barracks_armory_success: You successfully make it to the armory.
story.barracks_fight: The orcs immediately notice you and prepare to fight. Good luck.
story.barracks_quarters: You try to sneak to the living quarters.
story.barracks_quarters_fail: When you open the door to the first living area, you find a goblin in a dress. He screams and alerts the orcs.
story.barracks_quarters_success: You open the door and find it empty. You search around for anything useful.
story.barracks_combat_victory: The last orc falls in the fighting pit.
story.residential_enter: You continue on to the residential district.
story.residential:
You see a row of houses and hear noises coming from some of them, but you cannot tell how many enemies are inside.

You have a few choices:

Large Manor - You hear crashing and yelling.
Small Shack - It seems quiet, but doubtful to have anything of value.
Large House - It is in perfect condition. You do not hear anything, and through the windows you can only see a few chests. You feel a slight sense of power coming from the house.
Ignore the houses and go straight to the bridge.
story.residential_rest:
You find a defensible corner near the road to the bridge and take a final breather.

Your health is restored. There is no more time to linger.
story.large_manor: You open the door and find two orcs directing a squad of five goblins to ransack the place and look for something.
story.large_manor_unseen: They have not seen you yet. What do you do?
story.large_manor_grouped: They are all crowded together, whispering about something. This is the perfect chance to take them all out.
story.manor_sneak: They have not seen you yet. What do you want to do next?
story.manor_group_kill_warrior:
You notice that none of them are paying attention. You scoff and think, "Guess my drill sergeant was right. Complacency kills."

With large, barreling steps, you run over to the group, who finally look up at you. But it is too late. You swing your massive axe through all of their bodies, bisecting tops from bottoms.
story.manor_group_kill_scholar:
Academically, you know that it is a sign of intelligence to focus on what you are working on, but you cannot help thinking the group in front of you are complete morons for ignoring their surroundings in a place like this.

Oh well. Not your problem. They look up in horror as you quick-cast a fireball that melts the skin from their bodies and kills them before they have the chance to raise arms against you.
story.manor_group_kill_ranger:
You are used to your prey not noticing you as you move silently. This is no different. You scan the room and see an angle where you can take them out quickly. If luck is with you, and it usually is, you should be able to kill them before they realize they have become the hunted.

You ready five arrows, nock the first one, slowly take aim, draw, and release. Draw and release. Draw and release. Draw and release. Draw and release. Five dead in the span of a breath.
story.manor_group_kill_dwarf:
"Well, who am I to ignore a gift from Moradin?"

You saunter over to the group and, without hesitation, knock the two orcs' heads together hard enough to daze a golem, kick a goblin in the stomach, grab another goblin by the throat, and use him to beat the other one to death.

You curb-stomp the first goblin and finish him off, then ensure the orcs will not be able to come after you later.
story.manor_group_kill_dm: The monsters are so focused on their search that you simply decide they are done being useful. They collapse before they understand the scene has changed.
story.manor_combat_victory: The last monster falls in the ruined manor.
story.manor_win: Bloodied and exhausted, you stand victorious. Now to see if this place held anything worth killing for.
story.manor_loot: You ransack the house and find a set of unmarked keys, a small black chest, and a newspaper detailing that the king has enacted a draft to support the war effort.
story.manor_store_chest: You store the unmarked keys and the black chest for later. Whatever is inside can wait.
story.manor_chest_keys: The keys are unmarked. One of them must fit the chest.
story.manor_chest_trap: The chest was trapped and exploded with the force of a dragon's anus after a night of dwarven ale and a Crunch Roll Peasant Bell.
story.manor_chest_unlock: You unlocked the chest and notice that it was trapped. You do not want to imagine what would have happened if you had put the wrong key in.
story.manor_magistone_orb: You open the chest to find a pure magistone orb that seems to be pulsing with power.
story.small_shack: You open the door to find a gremlin and a ghoul connected in some kind of... uh, ritual. They notice you and attack.
story.small_shack_win:
The gremlin and ghoul fall, leaving the shack quiet and deeply uncomfortable.

There is nothing worth carrying out of here.
story.mimic_house:
You open the door to find a large room with tables, chairs, and a bar. Everything is exceptionally clean. Looking around, you see three chests spread out around the room.

A welcoming feeling invades your body. You feel that this is a safe place.
story.mimic_house_burn:
Being the little pyro that you are, you cast the low-level fire spell you learned as a child and set the house ablaze.

You walk away from a potentially disastrous situation and head to the bridge.
story.mimic_house_leave: You ignore the welcoming feeling, shut the door, and start walking away.
story.mimic_house_loot:
You go to the first chest and gleefully open it, only to see a chest full of shiny white teeth.

It is a mimic!
story.mimic_house_loot_clear: Your head suddenly clears, the welcoming sense fades, and you initiate combat with the mimic.
story.mimic_house_rest:
You set out your mat and lie down to rest. You are a lot more tired than you thought, and soon you fall fast asleep.

You wake up to three mimics eating your limbs, but you feel no pain. You look around and see that the inside of the house has morphed into the inside of a mouth. Acid coats the floor and slowly eats you away.
story.mimic_house_fight_one_win: The first mimic collapses with a wet, splintering groan.
story.mimic_house_second_wave: The other two mimics come alive and attack.
story.mimic_house_fight_two_win: The last mimic collapses in on itself, its splintered body twisting tighter and tighter until it forms a pulsing magistone orb. A torn shred of map slips free from the wreckage.
story.mimic_house_escape:
You take the magistone orb and the torn bridge map, then stumble out of the mimic house, bruised, slimed, and somehow alive.

The map reveals the first two directions to follow on the trapped bridge: LEFT, then UP.
story.mimic_house_flee:
You turn and run to the front door, but it is locked. The inside of the house starts to slowly morph. The walls take on a pink hue and green liquid starts to leak. The floor has taken on a squishy appearance and feel. The back of the house, where the back door was, starts to elongate until all you can see is a black hole. You smell something awful and look down; the green substance has started eating away at your boots.

The house is a mimic!
story.mimic_house_black_hole: You choose the black hole. This is exactly as bad an idea as it sounds.
story.mimic_house_fire:
The flame catches the wet inner wall. The mimic screeches and spits you out into the street.

You stand up, disoriented but alive.
story.mimic_house_walls:
The walls are hard as steel, and the mimic seems unaffected.

The house squeezes tighter.
story.mimic_house_ale:
The 3000-proof dwarven ale is too much for the mimic to handle. It instantly gags and vomits you onto the street.

You turn back as it stands on long, skinny legs and wobbles away.
story.bridge:
You stand before a massive bridge leading toward the King's Castle.

It stretches across a lake of molten lava, its stone ribs disappearing into ash and smoke. Magistone lanterns line the sides, casting a dim blue glow over broken wagons, shattered barricades, and old dwarven defenses.

Orcs and goblins patrol the span in loose groups. Crossing will mean moving between shadows, hiding behind debris, and staying quiet whenever possible.
story.bridge_has_map:
The old map showed a safe route through the bridge traps, but the parchment has crumbled from being handled too much.
You will need to follow the directions from memory.
story.bridge_partial_map: The torn map reveals only the first two safe directions: LEFT, then UP. After that, you will have to trust your memory and nerve.
story.bridge_no_map: Without a map or memorized directions, it may be impossible to safely cross.
story.bridge_temp_end:
Congratulations for making it this far.

The game is still in development, but the opening route through Visteria now reaches the bridge ritual and the silver mask choice.

Completed so far:
- Character selection and DM opening
- Caravan attack and escape routes
- Cave, ghost girl, and doll choices
- Barracks and merchant district routes
- Residential district encounters
- Manor and mimic house rewards
- Lava bridge approach, patrols, warehouse ritual, and silver mask decision

Still planned:
- Consequences of the silver mask
- Orc camp
- Castle
- Throne room
- Final boss fight
- Treasure room
- Spanish language translation
story.bridge_end_rest:
You find a sheltered stretch of bridge between two dim magistone lanterns and finally let yourself breathe.

The heat never fades, and the silence behind you feels wrong, but for now you are alive.

Your health is restored.
story.orc_camps_preview:
You leave the empty stretch of bridge behind and continue toward the far side.

Beyond the smoke, drums begin to sound from the orc camps.

Chapter complete for now. Future updates will continue from here.
story.bridge_prepare_success: You bind wounds and steady your hands.
story.bridge_sneak_start:
You step onto the bridge and move with the smoke.

Boots scrape against stone somewhere ahead. Goblins mutter near a lantern. An orc turns just as you slip behind a cracked slab of bridgework.
story.bridge_sneak_success: You pass between patrols, ducking from shadow to shadow until the bridge widens around a warehouse built into the structure itself.
story.bridge_sneak_fail: A goblin spots movement and shrieks. The nearest patrol turns toward you with weapons raised.
story.bridge_patrol_defeated: The patrol falls. Their bodies vanish into the smoke as the bridge rumbles beneath your feet.
story.warehouse_enter:
About a quarter of the way across, you find a warehouse built into the bridge structure.

Inside, a ritual is already in progress. A man wearing a silver mask stands at the center, leading the chant. Four cultists in robes and bronze masks surround the ritual circle while five orcs guard the room.

Every instinct in your body screams the same thing: this ritual must be stopped at all costs.
story.warehouse_cultists_defeated: The last orc drops. The bronze-masked cultists break one by one, and the ritual circle sputters like a dying flame.
story.warehouse_leader_rage:
The silver-masked leader turns toward you.

"You! You have it. I need it. Give it to me!"

His voice tears into a snarl. The mask flashes, and he flies into a rage.
story.warehouse_leader_defeated: The masked leader collapses beside the broken ritual circle. For a moment, everything is quiet except the lava groaning beneath the bridge.
story.silver_mask_choice:
The silver mask does not fall like dead metal.

It rests in the ash, untouched by blood or heat, and your eyes keep returning to it. Power radiates from its surface in slow, patient waves. It feels ancient. It feels dangerous.

It also feels like it is waiting for you.
story.silver_mask_leave_confirm:
You turn away, but the pull remains behind your eyes.

Are you absolutely sure you want to leave the silver mask behind?
story.silver_mask_left: You leave the silver mask in the ash and force yourself toward the far side of the warehouse.
story.silver_mask_stored: You wrap the silver mask carefully and store it away. Even hidden from sight, it feels heavier than it should.
story.silver_mask_worn:
You lift the silver mask and press it to your face.

Cold power floods your body. Your wounds tighten. Your grip strengthens. The world sharpens until every breath of smoke, every spark of lava, every distant footstep feels painfully clear.

Health +6. Damage +3.

For now, the mask is silent.
story.warehouse_ritual_surges:
The silver mask grows cold in your hands.

For a brief moment, silence fills the warehouse.

Then the ritual circle pulses.

Once.

Twice.

The blood markings spread across the floor like living veins as the air itself begins to vibrate. The remaining candles flare violently, their flames twisting into unnatural shapes.

The ritual is still active.

And it is growing stronger.

A low sound begins to echo from the center of the circle. Not a voice. Not quite. More like reality itself groaning under pressure.

The center of the ritual slowly tears open.

At first it is no larger than a coin, suspended in the air like a crack in glass. Then it widens. Darkness spills from within, deeper than shadow, swallowing the light around it.

Heat pours from the opening.

Something moves inside.

Your instincts scream at you to act.

What do you do?
story.warehouse_leave:
You turn and sprint for the exit as the warehouse trembles behind you.

The magistone lanterns outside begin to flicker violently as a deafening roar erupts from within the structure. The bridge itself groans beneath your feet.

You risk a glance backward.

The hole has expanded to consume nearly the entire ritual chamber.

Then something emerges.

A massive ashen-gray head forces itself through the opening, its maw splitting unnaturally wide with rows of jagged teeth grinding against one another.

Then another head appears.

And another.

Seven grotesque heads writhe and snap as they drag themselves from the darkness beyond. Their malformed body twists unnaturally, supported by massive pincers and disturbingly human-like hands that claw against the stone floor.

The creature pulls itself fully into the world.
story.warehouse_mask_whisper_stayed:
And somewhere deep inside your mind, you hear the mask whisper:

"You should have stayed."
story.false_hydra_interruption:
The creature's seven heads screech in unison as molten saliva drips from their jagged mouths onto the stone bridge below.

The warehouse collapses around it as the abomination drags itself fully into the world. Human-like hands claw against the ground while massive pincers tear through wood and steel as if they were parchment.

Every instinct in your body screams the same thing.

Run.

Or fight.

No matter your choice...

Everything stops.

The creature freezes mid-motion.

The falling ash hangs motionless in the air.

Even the lava below the bridge stills into perfect silence.

The world itself feels as though it is holding its breath.

Then you feel it.

A presence.

Cold.

Ancient.

Watching.
story.order_mask_warning:
The silver mask suddenly feels impossibly heavy.

An overwhelming instinct crashes into your mind.

Hide it.

Whatever is approaching...

It wants the mask.
story.order_arrival:
Slow footsteps echo through the warehouse entrance.

One by one, figures emerge from the shadows.

Tall.

Armored.

Each wears gleaming white mithril-like armor draped in deep purple cloaks. Their faces are hidden behind flawless white masks devoid of expression.

There are twelve of them.

The figure leading them stands taller than the others, his cloak dragging across the broken stone floor as he approaches the frozen creature without even acknowledging its presence.

Then his masked face turns toward you.

"It appears," he says calmly, "that you have discovered something particularly nasty."

You cannot tell whether he means the monster...

Or something else.
story.order_dialogue_who:
The figure folds his hands behind his back.

"You stand before the Order of Lucius."

His tone carries the same weight as someone announcing the sunrise.

"We are the hand that restores order when lesser beings fail to do so."

One of the masked warriors behind him lowers their head slightly in reverence at the mention of the name.

"Where chaos festers, we purge it. Where corruption spreads, we burn it away."

Though his words sound noble...

The way he speaks makes it painfully clear that he views you as insignificant.
story.order_dialogue_monster:
The masked figure slowly turns toward the frozen abomination.

"A False Hydra."

Even saying the name seems to darken the air.

"A parasite upon reality itself. It feeds upon memory, identity, and thought. Entire cities have vanished beneath their song."

One of the creature's heads twitches slightly despite time itself appearing frozen.

The man does not react.

"It should not exist here."

For the first time, his voice carries the faintest trace of irritation.
story.order_dialogue_why:
"Because this world requires order."

The response comes immediately.

"This creature threatens the balance established by Lucius."

He slowly walks past you toward the frozen monster.

"And so we will remove it."

There is no compassion in his voice.

No fear.

No hesitation.

Only certainty.

The figure pauses briefly before continuing.

"The will of Lucius is not questioned. It is obeyed."

Something about the way he says it sends a chill through your entire body.
story.order_departure:
The masked figure studies you silently for a moment.

Then he speaks.

"You would do well to leave this place."

His tone is calm, yet absolute. Not a suggestion.

A command.

Behind him, the eleven masked warriors spread out across the ruined warehouse with practiced precision while the frozen False Hydra remains suspended in place like a grotesque statue.

The figure turns away from you as though the conversation is already over.

"This matter belongs to the Order now."

For reasons you cannot fully explain...

You listen.

You back away from the warehouse entrance before finally turning and making your way down the bridge. The heat from the molten lake below presses against your skin while distant echoes of chanting still linger in your mind.

Then suddenly...

*POP*

The sound is small.

Sharp.

Wrong.

You immediately turn around.

The warehouse is gone.

The ritual circle is gone.

The portal is gone.

The False Hydra is gone.

Even the damage from the battle has vanished.

Only an empty stretch of bridge remains behind you, illuminated by rows of softly glowing magistone lanterns.

It is as if nothing ever existed there at all.

No blood.

No bodies.

No sign that anyone had ever been present.

A cold feeling settles into your stomach.
story.order_mask_missed_one:
The silver mask beneath your cloak feels warm.

And for just a brief moment...

You swear you hear whispering.

"They missed one."
story.silver_mask_reworn:
Your hands move before you fully decide to move them.

You put the silver mask back on.

The cold weight settles over your face, and a breath you did not know you were holding leaves your chest. Relief rolls through you first. Then power.

The bridge feels quieter with the mask in place.
story.silver_mask_put_on_after_order:
You take out the silver mask and put it on.

The moment it touches your face, the fear in your chest loosens. Strength rushes through your limbs, cold and clean and far too welcome.

Health +6. Damage +3.

For now, the mask is silent.
story.warehouse_stop_ritual:
You lunge for the ritual circle and drive your weapon through the nearest line of living blood.

The mark splits. The circle screams without a mouth. For one terrible second, the widening hole strains against you, trying to pull the warehouse and everything in it into the dark.

Then the circle buckles. The opening collapses to a burning slit, still alive, still hungry, but no longer spreading.

Whatever was coming through has been delayed. Not defeated. Delayed.
story.warehouse_jump_hole:
You jump into the hole.

For an instant, there is heat, pressure, and the feeling of falling in every direction at once.

Then the world closes behind you.
story.warehouse_fail: The battle breaks you against the warehouse floor.
story.bridge_navigation_start: You step onto the trapped bridge. Choose the first direction.
story.bridge_wrong:
The stone under your foot sinks with a hollow click.
The bridge trap opens beneath you.
story.bridge_success: You follow the final direction and reach the far side without waking the traps.
story.production:
The production area is a maze of chains, hooks, furnaces, and dead machines.
One wrong turn will end you.

Choose the first path.
story.production_success:
You find the hidden maintenance lift and ride it toward moonlight.
Chapter complete. You have escaped the buried city of Visteria.
story.production_fail: The floor gives way beneath you. The production area keeps its secrets.
story.complete: You have completed this playable chapter of Tales of Visteria.
story.game_over_footer:
Thanks for playing.
This was made in memory of a DnD session with friends.
game_over.hydra:
The hydra was not an encounter. It was a weather event with teeth.
The story ends before anyone can object.
game_over.forest_mind_break:
The forest repeats one time too many.
Your thoughts unravel into gray miasma, and you wander beneath the same trees for the rest of your life.
game_over.ghost_black_pits:
The pale girl's eyes become empty black pits.
You lose your sense of self, then the dark takes the rest.
game_over.combat:
The fight turns against you.
Steel, teeth, and panic close in until Visteria falls silent again.
game_over.warehouse:
The warehouse ritual notices you.
Whatever waits inside that violet light accepts your life as payment.
game_over.ritual_hole: The ritual opening accepts you before you understand what waits beyond it.
game_over.bridge_trap:
The bridge defenses wake beneath your feet.
Without the right path, the old city claims you as another warning.
game_over.production:
The production tunnels shift, bite, and open beneath you.
The buried city keeps its secrets.
game_over.mimic:
The chest was not treasure. It was hunger pretending to be useful.
Your adventure ends between wooden teeth.
game_over.manor_chest:
The black chest detonates before you can let go of the key.
The buried city keeps one more secret, and you become part of the wreckage.
game_over.default:
You fall in the dark beneath Visteria.
Start a new game to try another route.
